senses.definition: used to begin a statement that may disagree with or criticize someone while still being polite

examples.x: With all due respect, I have a different opinion on how to approach this project.
With all due respect, I don't think your proposal addresses the main concerns raised in the meeting.
With all due respect, I feel that your statement oversimplifies a complex issue.
With all due respect, I question whether this strategy aligns with our long-term goals.
With all due respect, I think we should reconsider our position on this matter.

senses.definition: used to give enthusiastic permission or encouragement to do something

examples.x: If you want to join us for dinner, by all means, feel free to come over.
If you have any questions about the project, by all means, don't hesitate to ask.
By all means, let's explore new opportunities for collaboration between our departments.
If you'd like to try the new recipe, by all means, I'll send it to you right away.
By all means, take the day off if you're feeling unwell—we'll manage without you.

senses.definition: used to indicate that what is about to be mentioned is so obvious that it does not require further explanation

examples.x: Needless to say, a fire alarm should be taken seriously, and everyone should evacuate the building immediately when it goes off.
Needless to say, safety should always be our top priority in any construction project.
She's been working here for over 10 years, so needless to say, she's very experienced.
We've invested a lot of time and resources into this project, so needless to say, we're expecting great results.
We're in the middle of a global pandemic, so needless to say, travel plans have been put on hold for many people.

senses.definition: used to introduce a statement or idea that should be considered because of the information or context just discussed

examples.x: We have a limited budget for this project, so with that in mind, we need to prioritize our expenses carefully.

senses.definition: used to indicate that the statement being made is a significant understatement or that there is much more that could be said about the topic

examples.x: The traffic during rush hour was a nightmare, to say the least.
His behavior at the meeting was inappropriate, to say the least—he was rude and disrespectful to everyone present.
The movie was disappointing, to say the least.
His performance in the game was lackluster, to say the least—he made several critical errors that cost us the match.
The situation is concerning, to say the least.

senses.definition: used to emphasize that the speaker is being sincere and truthful in their statement

examples.x: In all honesty, I believe this is the best solution to our problem.
In all honesty, I don't think the project will be completed on time without additional resources.
In all honesty, I wasn't impressed with the quality of the service we received at the restaurant.
In all honesty, I think we need to have a candid discussion about our relationship and where it's headed.
In all honesty, I'm worried about the direction the company is heading in—it seems like we're losing focus.

senses.definition: used to indicate that something is so obvious that it does not need to be explicitly mentioned

examples.x: It goes without saying that hard work is essential for success.
It goes without saying that safety should always be our top priority in any workplace.
It goes without saying that honesty is crucial in any meaningful relationship.
It goes without saying that studying regularly is essential for academic success.
It goes without saying that taking care of our environment is important for future generations.

senses.definition: used to emphasize that the speaker is about to be completely honest in what they are going to say

examples.x: To tell the truth, I didn't enjoy the movie as much as I thought I would.
To tell the truth, I wasn't impressed with the quality of the product.
To tell the truth, I'm not sure if I can meet the deadline given the current workload.
To tell the truth, I've been feeling overwhelmed lately with everything going on.
To tell the truth, I've been struggling with this decision for a while now.

senses.definition: used to emphasize that the speaker is revealing something honestly or openly

examples.x: Truth be told, I was nervous before giving the presentation.
Truth be told, I've never been very good at keeping secrets.
Truth be told, I'm not sure if I'm ready for the responsibilities that come with this promotion.
Truth be told, I've been struggling with anxiety for a while now.
Truth be told, I'm not sure if I can trust him after what happened last time.
